content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation p1,p2,…,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1​,p2​,…,pN​ of the integers 111 through NNN. You may perform up to KKK operations of the following type:


Choose three pairwise distinct valid indices i1,i2,i3i_1, i_2, i_3i1​,i2​,i3​. Note that these indices do not have to be chosen in increasing order.
Let's denote the values of pi1p_{i_1}pi1​​, pi2p_{i_2}pi2​​ and pi3p_{i_3}pi3​​ before this operation by v1v_1v1​, v2v_2v2​ and v3v_3v3​ respectively.
Change pi2p_{i_2}pi2​​ to v1v_1v1​, pi3p_{i_3}pi3​​ to v2v_2v2​ and pi1p_{i_1}pi1​​ to v3v_3v3​. In other words, perform a cyclic right shift on the elements pi1,pi2,pi3p_{i_1}, p_{i_2}, p_{i_3}pi1​​,pi2​​,pi3​​ in this order.
For example, the permutation (1,4,3,2,5)(1,4,3,2,5)(1,4,3,2,5) can be changed to the permutation (1,5,3,4,2)(1,5,3,4,2)(1,5,3,4,2) in one operation by choosing i1=2i_1 = 2i1​=2, i2=4i_2 = 4i2​=4 and i3=5i_3 = 5i3​=5.

Your task is to sort the permutation in increasing order. Note that you do not have to minimise the number of performed operations.
constraints: 1≤pi≤N1 \le p_i \le N1≤pi​≤N for each valid iii
p1,p2,…,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1​,p2​,…,pN​ are pairwise distinct
the sum of all NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105
pi=N−i+1p_i = N-i+1pi​=N−i+1 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers p1,p2,…,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1​,p2​,…,pN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If it is impossible to sort the given permutation using at most KKK operations, print a single line containing the integer −1-1−1.
Otherwise, first, print a line containing a single integer MMM (0≤M≤K0 \le M \le K0≤M≤K) ― the number of operations you want to perform.
Then, print MMM lines describing these operations. For each iii (1≤i≤M1 \le i \le M1≤i≤M), the iii-th of these lines should contain three space-separated integers aia_iai​, bib_ibi​ and cic_ici​ (pairwise distinct; 1≤ai,bi,ci≤N1 \le a_i, b_i, c_i \le N1≤ai​,bi​,ci​≤N) ― the indices on which the iii-th operation is performed.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We can sort the permutation by performing one cyclic right shift operation on the indices 111, 333 and 444.
